reason: Remove navigators
Have a single color type
Have a single origin 3D type
Have a single extent 3D type
Remove gpu-supported-limits.copy-external-image-to-texture as it's web related
Remove gpu-device.import-external-texture as it's web related
Remove bases that are never used

Beautify gpu-*-binding.set-bind-group, no tuple params. Note: offset & length are not optional in JS, but seems to be JS specific reason for this https://github.com/gpuweb/gpuweb/pull/440#discussion_r342299860
Beautify gpu-command-encoder.copy-buffer-to-buffer, no tuple params. Using the more powerful variant with offsets and making the offsets optional
Remove allow-shared-buffer-source as it's web related and temporarily replace it with list<u8>
Change predefined-color-space to enum
Remove prefixes of identifiers with leading digits
Simplify naming of gpu-binding-resource
Simplify naming of gpu-layout-mode, and merge gpu-auto-layout-mode cases into gpu-layout-mode
Add get root function
Remove array-buffer and replace it temporarily with list<u8>
GPURenderPassLayout is only used as the base of GPURenderBundleEncoderDescriptor. Since wit doesn't have inheritance the fields are duplicated in the child so no need for the parent.

Accepting a texture in place of a texture view is just shorthand for texture.createView() (https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#abstract-opdef-get-as-texture-view), so it's unnecessary — callers can call create-view themselves. JS can hide this behind dynamic typing, but in WIT it would require a variant and complicate the API. webgpu.h also disallows it (https://github.com/gpuweb/gpuweb/pull/5228#issuecomment-3046161091).

Convert WebIDL-style flag namespaces (a resource holding static funcs that return a u32 typedef) into native WIT `flags` types. WebIDL uses this shape because JS has no typed bit-flags primitive; WIT does, so we use it. Also removes the now-unused `gpu-flags-constant` alias.

Errors have the message field raised to the top level type so that they don't have to be taken out of the inner error. Important if we allow new error types in the future that the developer has not yet handled. They also get a `kind` to enable detecting the error type. `kind` is a variant and not an enum so that they can have fields inside like `pipeline-error.reason`. Note: not changing gpu-error to a record even though errors added by us are records, because GPUError is an interface in the spec, and might one day gain interface like properties

Remove gpu-uncaptured-error-event constructor as it's not useful, it's only here because of web specific rules. https://github.com/gpuweb/gpuweb/pull/255#issuecomment-477308123 and https://w3ctag.github.io/design-principles/#constructors
request-device can throw for various reasons. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpuadapter-requestdevice
create-*-pipeline-async throws if pipeline creation fails. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#async-pipeline-creation
create-query-set throws if descriptor.type is 'timestamp', but 'timestamp-query' is not enabled for this device. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpudevice-createqueryset
pop-error-scope throws if the error-scope stack is empty. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpudevice-poperrorscope
on-uncaptured-error should not be one long word and is of event type is gpu-error https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#telemetry
map-async can throw for various reasons. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpubuffer-mapasync
get-mapped-range can throw for various reasons. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpubuffer-getmappedrange
unmap throws if called while map-async is still pending. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpubuffer-unmap

set-bind-group throws if dynamic-offsets-data-start/length don't fit in dynamic-offsets-data. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpubindingcommandsmixin-setbindgroup-index-bindgroup-dynamicoffsetsdata-dynamicoffsetsdatastart-dynamicoffsetsdatalength
write-buffer can throw for various reasons. https://www.w3.org/TR/webgpu/#dom-gpuqueue-writebuffer
